data.title: Coinbase and its executives are sued by shareholders, alleging they misled investors about bankruptcy risks and violated securities laws
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase shareholder Wenduo Guo filed a lawsuit in the New Jersey Federal Court on February 18, accusing Coinbase and its leadership of failing to disclose that customer assets may be considered part of Coinbase's bankruptcy property, making retail customers unsecured creditors, thereby misleading investors about bankruptcy risks and violating securities laws.
The lawsuit claims that before Coinbase went public in April 2021, at least 75 cryptocurrency exchanges collapsed, leaving these customers unable to recover their digital assets, "despite repeated statements from the company's management to the contrary, Coinbase is no different in terms of the risk of losing digital assets in bankruptcy."
The complaint also claims that Coinbase failed to disclose that it was engaging in proprietary trading to make up for the decline in cryptocurrency prices, which is a "dangerous practice of using company funds to trade assets." In addition, executives including CEO Brian Armstrong (named in the lawsuit) sold large quantities of stock and made hundreds of millions of dollars in personal profits from it. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Wintermute plans to expand in the U.S. and open an office in New York
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Cryptocurrency market maker Wintermute Trading Ltd. is considering expanding its business to the United States, including opening an office in New York and offering new products in the U.S. Wintermute is one of the largest liquidity providers in the digital asset market and already has offices in London and Singapore. CEO Evgeny Gaevoy described the company's growth plans and "new focus on the United States" in an interview during the Consensus conference in Hong Kong. Gaevoy said that as part of the expansion plan, the company plans to provide OTC products to U.S. customers and add about 5 to 10 people to the existing team of 130. Wintermute recently raised funds in a round of financing led by Tencent Holdings Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter. Neither Tencent nor Wintermute responded to requests for comment on this round of financing. The company previously completed a $20 million Series B financing led by Lightspeed Ventures in January 2021, with participation from Pantera Capital and Hack VC. (Bloomberg)

data.title: BNB Surpasses 650 USDT with a Narrowed 2.29% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 19, 2025, 03:52 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 650 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 650.119995 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 2.29% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: VARA To Enhance Global Regulatory Collaboration In Virtual Assets
data.description: According to Foresight News, during the Consensus Hong Kong 2025 conference, Matthew White, CEO of the Dubai Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA), announced plans to strengthen collaboration with international regulatory bodies in the virtual assets sector. White highlighted that stablecoins have revolutionized the global financial system and payment networks, while also expanding the accessibility of the U.S. dollar.

data.title: The crypto market sector continued to pull back, AI Agents fell 9.53%, and the RWA sector was relatively strong
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News On February 19, according to SoSoValue data, the crypto market sector continued to pull back, AI Agents fell 9.53%, and the RWA sector was relatively strong. In the AI ​​Agents sector, Virtuals Protocol (VIRTUAL), AIXBT, ai16z (AI16Z), and AI Rig Complex (ARC) fell by 13.96%, 10.63%, 11.22%, and 18.59% in 24 hours, respectively. However, the RWA sector rose by 1.72% in 24 hours, among which MANTRA (OM), Maker (MKR), and Plume (PLUME) rose by 4.35%, 10.63%, and 9.53%, respectively.
In addition, the Meme sector fell by 3.47% in 24 hours. In the sector, Dogecoin (DOGE) fell by 2.37%, Shiba Inu (SHIB) fell by 2.75%, and Pepe (PEPE) fell by 5.76%. It is worth noting that the total market value of the Meme sector fell to the level of November 2024, and the winter gains were almost completely vacated.
In other sectors, the CeFi sector fell 3.25% in 24 hours, the PayFi sector fell 3.30%, but Litecoin (LTC) rose 6.55% against the trend. The Layer1 sector fell 3.67%, and the DeFi sector fell 4.22%.

data.title: Trump To Sign Executive Order This Wednesday
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump is set to sign an executive order this Wednesday at 19:30 Eastern Time. This development comes amid various market speculations. Further details regarding the content of the executive order have not been disclosed.

data.title: Semler Scientific Q4 net income rises to nearly $300 million as it adopts new accounting rules to account for Bitcoin holdings
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Semler Scientific (SMLR), a US-listed company, adopted a new "fair value" accounting rule to measure the company's digital asset holdings in the fourth quarter of 2024, increasing the value of its Bitcoin reserves by $28.85 million, resulting in total net income of $29.206 billion or $3.64 per share for the quarter, compared to $4.2 million or $0.62 in the same period last year.
The FASB fair value rule was introduced in March 2024, requiring companies to report digital assets at market value as of the first quarter of 2025. However, companies can adopt the rule before then at their own discretion.
As of today, Semler continues to hold 3,192 BTC, worth more than $300 million. The company's total purchase price is $280.4 million. (CoinDesk)
